mission.title: Install and Configure K8gb on Kubernetes
mission.description: K8GB is a cloud-native Kubernetes Global Balancer that provides a solution for global service load balancing across multiple clusters. Installing K8GB allows you to manage traffic efficiently and ensure high availability for your applications.

steps.title: Add the K8GB Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the K8GB Helm chart repository to your Helm client to access the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo add k8gb https://charts.k8gb.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install K8GB using Helm
steps.description:
Install K8GB in a new namespace called 'k8gb' with the specified version.
```bash
helm install k8gb k8gb/k8gb --namespace k8gb --create-namespace --version v0.18.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the K8GB pods are running successfully in the 'k8gb' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n k8gb
```

steps.title: Configure Resource Limits
steps.description:
Edit the K8GB deployment to set resource limits for better performance and stability.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment k8gb -n k8gb
# Add the following under spec.template.spec.containers.resources:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   requests:
#     cpu: "250m"
#     memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up a Global Load Balancer
steps.description:
Create a sample GSLB resource to manage your services globally.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8gb.absa.oss/v1beta1
kind: Gslb
metadata:
  name: hello-world-gslb
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    k8gb.io/hostname: "hello.lb.mycompany.com"
spec:
  resourceRef:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    matchLabels:
      app: hello-world
  strategy:
    type: failover
    primaryGeoTag: "eu"
    dnsTtlSeconds: 30
  splitBrainThresholdSeconds: 300
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of K8GB will show the K8GB pods running in the 'k8gb' namespace. You can also verify the GSLB resource is created and configured correctly by checking the status of your services.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add k8gb https://charts.k8gb.io
helm repo update
helm install k8gb k8gb/k8gb --namespace k8gb --create-namespace --version v0.18.1

kubectl edit deployment k8gb -n k8gb
# Add the following under spec.template.spec.containers.resources:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   requests:
#     cpu: "250m"
#     memory: "256Mi"

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8gb.absa.oss/v1beta1
kind: Gslb
metadata:
  name: hello-world-gslb
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    k8gb.io/hostname: "hello.lb.mycompany.com"
spec:
  resourceRef:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    matchLabels:
      app: hello-world
  strategy:
    type: failover
    primaryGeoTag: "eu"
    dnsTtlSeconds: 30
  splitBrainThresholdSeconds: 300
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
